anchorText: Till age snow white hairs on thee

levels.body: "The objective case of the pronoun thou, representing the Old English accusative and dative."
worksCited: Weiner, E. S. C., and J. A. Simpson. *The Oxford English Dictionary*. 2nd ed. Oxford: Clarendon, 	1989. Print.

levels.body: Referring to the line above (Line 12) "Ride ten thousand days and night" is another statement to point out that it takes a lifetime to find someone who is "true and fair" - namely until one's hair is white like that of an old person.
